DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF JOSHUA STEINHAUER, PATTY GLATT'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS PERTAINING TO PLAINTIFF STEINHAUER'S MEDICAL HISTORY; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS)THE MOTION TO QUASH IS DENIED WITH TWO EXCEPTIONS: (1) THE REQUEST FOR BILLING RECORDS IS LIMITED TO OCTOBER 2005 TO THE PRESENT, PER DEFENDANT'S OFFER; AND (2) THE SUBPOENAS SHALL EXCLUDE PLAINTIFF'S PSYCHOLOGICAL RECORDS, PROVIDED THAT NO CLAIM IS BEING MADE FOR MENTAL AND EMOTIONAL DISTRESS OVER AND ABOVE THAT USUALLY ASSOCIATED WITH THE PHYSICAL INJURIES CLAIMED, PER PLAINTIFF'S OFFER. THE PRODUCTION DATE ON THE REISSUED SUBPOENAS WILL BE AT LEAST 45 DAYS TO ALLOW THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING A STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER TO LIMIT THE INTRUSION ON PLAINTIFF'S PRIVACY INTEREST. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE PRO TEM: AARON MINNIS, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-01-2019 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-15-2018 Continued To Aug-05-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Pertaining To Plaintiff Steinhauer'S Medical History; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Greg O'Connell, Esq. 415-341-9341 for Plaintiff.
John C. Won, Esq. 925-838-1100 of Craddick, Candland & Conti for Defendant Charles E. Skomer.

Both sides stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem hearing the matter.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOSHUA STEINHAUER, PATTY GLATT'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Pertaining To Plaintiff Steinhauer'S Medical History; Request For Sanctions.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff has a privacy interest in his medical information. However, defendant has demonstrated that plaintiff's medical information is relevant to the injuries put at issue by plaintiff in his complaint and discovery responses. Complaint 3, 20, 25, 48; Won Decl., Ex. 1, Nos. 6.2, 6.3. Plaintiff has not met his burden to show feasible and effective alternatives to the subpoenas which have a lesser impact on privacy interests, and plaintiff's proposals to narrow the scope are limited to only some of the issues and injuries claimed. Therefore, plaintiff may choose to limit the injuries he puts at issue. Otherwise, the motion to quash is denied with two exceptions: (1) the request for billing records is limited to October 2005 to the present, per defendant's offer; and (2) the subpoenas shall exclude plaintiff's psychological records, provided that no claim is being made for mental and emotional distress over and above that usually associated with the physical injuries claimed, per plaintiff's offer. The production date on the reissued subpoenas will be at least 45 days to allow the parties to meet and confer regarding a stipulated protective order to limit the intrusion on plaintiff's privacy interest. Sanctions are denied.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-28-2018 Set For Trial On Mar-18-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
